title: Upon Time
content:
Time was upon
The wing, to fly away;
And I call'd on
Him but awhile to stay;
But he'd be gone,
For aught that I could say.

He held out then
A writing, as he went,
And ask'd me, when
False man would be content
To pay again
What God and Nature lent.

An hour-glass,
In which were sands but few,
As he did pass,
He shew'd, and told me too
Mine end near was;
And so away he flew.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Robert Herrick delivers a powerful performance in "Upon Time"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
